comment: Reference script chunked into caption units. Placeholder timing distributes each segment's units proportionally by word count; once real audio exists, whisper timings replace the proportional ones (text stays as written here — it is the cleaned source of truth).
s1: Most financial education fails for a simple reason:
it assumes people are struggling because they don't know enough.
They're struggling because the math of their lives doesn't work,
and nobody built tools for that.
I'm Scott Glasgow, founder of FinMango,
s2: We build free, open financial decision tools.
Calculators, simulations, and curriculum designed around real constraints,
A renter can find out in sixty seconds whether a lease is sustainable.
A first-gen student can compare retirement accounts without a finance degree.
No logins, no paywalls, works on any phone.
s3: Our research with Google Health has been used by the WHO, the World Bank, and the IMF.
And our Financial Health Barometer tracks household financial stress in near real time,
so we know where help is needed before the eviction filings show up.
s4: Here's why we scale: our tools are modular and open.
Reaching a new country means translating content, not rebuilding technology,
and we already have trained ambassadors in 12 countries ready to localize.
This year, 419 people applied for 11 ambassador spots.
The demand exists. The tools exist.
What we need is CGAP's distribution muscle and measurement expertise to connect them.
Pick FinMango, and you're not funding another literacy program.
You're funding infrastructure for financial health.
